J1.basis: This site pivoted from **InboxZap** (email-cleanup tool) to **Lumina** (AI meeting assistant) — a completely different product... All 12 existing blog posts target legacy InboxZap keywords... ~13,000 impressions combined but only 7 clicks total, because searchers have zero interest in a meeting assistant.
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot (live site = meeting assistant, GSC = legacy inbox demand, repo left in stale/broken state) and frames every recommendation around it, including preserving the branded InboxZap query still ranking #4.

J2.basis: **`next.config.js`** — 301-redirects all 12 legacy posts to `/blog`... ~13,000 impressions combined but only 7 clicks total
J2.rationale: Grounds the decision in real aggregate GSC numbers and applies a data-justified disposition (301 to /blog), but treats all 12 posts as one undifferentiated bucket with no asset-by-asset or tiered dispositions and no per-post impressions/positions cited.

J3.basis: 301-redirects all 12 legacy posts to `/blog` (content pruning after the pivot, consolidating equity instead of leaving dead-weight off-topic content indexed)... added an `Organization` JSON-LD with `alternateName: "InboxZap"` plus a footer mention, so legacy brand search equity transfers instead of being lost.
J3.rationale: Avoids both failure modes — neither doubles down on inbox content nor 404s the legacy URLs — preserving branded equity while building the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine; weaker on equity salvage since blanket 301s of off-topic posts to a bare index often soft-404, and the tension is implied rather than fully articulated.

J1.basis: This site pivoted from **InboxZap** (email-cleanup tool) to **Lumina** (AI meeting assistant)... All 12 existing blog posts target legacy InboxZap keywords... ~13,000 impressions combined but only 7 clicks total, because searchers have zero interest in a meeting assistant.
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, correctly reads the live product as the meeting assistant and the legacy blog impressions as dead inbox-cleanup demand, and frames every change around the new positioning. Falls just short of anchor_10's full 'repo is partially stale' articulation but is otherwise textbook.

J2.basis: **`next.config.js`** — 301-redirects all 12 legacy posts to `/blog`... Consolidate to the blog index rather than leaving them live or 404ing indexed URLs.
J2.rationale: It disposes of the legacy posts with reasoning and cites aggregate data (13k impressions / 7 clicks), but applies one blanket 301-to-index to all 12 rather than a per-asset or tiered decision, cites no individual impression/position figures, and harvests none of them into a relevant successor — so it lands between the blanket-instruction anchor and the asset-by-asset anchor.

J3.basis: the old brand name InboxZap had real remaining equity (its own branded query still ranks #4, 14 clicks)... so legacy brand search equity transfers instead of being lost. ... 301-redirects all 12 legacy posts to /blog (content pruning after the pivot, consolidating equity instead of leaving dead-weight off-topic content indexed).
J3.rationale: Avoids both failure modes — doesn't chase inbox keywords, and preserves brand equity via alternateName/FAQ/redirects while building the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine. Held back from a top score because the bridge for the 12 posts is a crude redirect-to-index rather than a sequenced transition, and the tension is implied more than explicitly articulated.

J1.basis: This site pivoted from **InboxZap** (email-cleanup tool) to **Lumina** (AI meeting assistant) — a completely different product... searchers have zero interest in a meeting assistant
J1.rationale: Explicitly nails the pivot, identifies GSC inbox impressions as legacy/off-topic demand, notes the codebase was left partially stale, and frames every change around the meeting-assistant reality.

J2.basis: 301-redirects all 12 legacy posts to `/blog` (content pruning after the pivot, consolidating equity...)
J2.rationale: Cites real aggregate GSC numbers (~13k impressions/7 clicks; branded query #4, 14 clicks) and issues a data-justified disposition, but applies one blanket 301 to all 12 posts rather than walking them asset-by-asset or in tiers — no per-post harvest/repurpose/keep decisions.

J3.basis: so legacy brand search equity transfers instead of being lost... 301-redirects all 12 legacy posts to /blog... 3 new blog posts targeting actual Lumina buyer-intent keywords
J3.rationale: Lands a defensible middle path — preserves branded equity via 301s, alternateName, footer and an explicit FAQ answer while building the meeting-assistant content cluster as the growth engine; the transition is bridged rather than torched or blindly chased.
